results.title: Principal Software Engineer at CarMax | Jeremy
highlights:
Principal Software Engineer at CarMax | Jeremy
...
# Principal Software Engineer
...
Principal engineer leading CarMax's Developer Experience platform, building agentic AI tooling, MCP ecosystems, and developer infrastructure for 500+ engineers.
...
Azure Git TypeScript C# Node.js GitHub.NET Azure DevOps NATS Distributed Systems Agile Microservices REST Telemetry IaC Agentic AI Authentication RAG SAML Platform Engineering
...
The Developer Experience team is the internal platform and enablement engine for CarMax Technology. Our mission is to make every engineer at CarMax faster, more confident, and better equipped through the tools, platforms, and practices they use every day. We own the developer portal, golden path frameworks, and the agentic AI tooling platform: MCP integrations, skills, agents, and the configuration infrastructure that makes AI-assisted development work at scale and is reshaping how software gets built here. We sit at the intersection of platform engineering and developer advocacy, and we treat the developer as our customer.
...
Build and maintain the integration layer that makes agentic AI development work at scale for 500+ engineers: MCP servers, skills, agent workflows, multi-model routing configuration, and the tooling instrumentation that lets the organization understand how engineers are working.
...
Principal Engineers within the Developer Experience team sit at the heart of how CarMax builds software. They are hands-on technical leaders who don’t just write excellent code, they shape the platforms and practices that multiply the effectiveness of every engineer in the organization. Recognized as technical authorities and platform advocates, they are responsible for building and evolving the systems that underpin developer productivity: the agentic AI tooling platform, MCP and skills ecosystem, developer portal, and the integrations that connect CarMax’s engineering environment to the AI tools engineers use every day.
...
It takes a genuine passion for developer experience to build the tools that make other engineers faster. Working collaboratively as part of the Engineering Experience group, you will engage in the full development lifecycle: understanding the real challenges developers face, designing platform solutions at scale, integrating emerging AI capabilities into the SDLC, and delivering outcomes that are measurable and durable. You balance developer needs with platform reliability and know how to ship technical solutions that compound in value over time. Creative, curious, and technically deep, you thrive at the intersection of platform engineering and AI innovation. You are energized by a landscape that keeps moving: new models, evolving specifications, and platform questions that do not yet have established answers. You never stop learning, and you bring others along as you go.
...
### AI & Developer Tooling
...
Agentic AI coding workflows in daily practice: you have built with these tools, not just read about them
...
Skills and agent workflow authoring: configuration-as-code for AI tools, slash commands, reusable workflow patterns
...
Multi-model environments: model selection by task type, cost and quality tradeoffs across frontier and open-weight models, routing configuration
...
Node.js / TypeScript: relevant to MCP server development and the broader tooling ecosystem alongside .NET
...
MCP development: protocol fundamentals (studio, SSE, Streamable HTTP), tool and resource definitions, local vs. remote deployment patterns, the CarMax wrapper pattern for third-party MCPs, and the context window and token cost discipline that separates a well-built MCP from one that degrades model performance
...
Familiarity with RAG architecture, vector search, or knowledge graph approaches, adjacent to active work in the Onramp AI enhancement space
...
Active engagement with the AI developer tooling community: you follow where this is going, not just where it has been

results.title: Genesis Montejo
highlights:
Software Engineer, GenAI @ CarMax | Driving Enterprise AI Solutions
...
Engineer II, AI & ML at [CarMax](https://www.linkedin.com/company/carmax)
...
👨‍💻 Hey! I'm Genesis, a data nerd turned software engineer who believes the answers to life's questions can be extracted from a Pandas DataFrame. 📈 I love collecting and transforming data for practical use in analysis, software, and predictive analytics. I’ve extracted insights from a variety of data sources and in various forms; one project involved using LLMs to scrape source code and article text from websites with varying HTML structures for a research paper on software security. 🛠️ I'm analytical by nature, but a builder at heart who enjoys creating systems and software that help users harness data in innovative ways. I’ve developed and deployed LLM-powered RAG chatbots with knowledge of 4 million proprietary data points. I’ve designed complex LLM systems with open-source frameworks along with chatbot interfaces, PySpark data pipelines, and self-hosted vector databases. 💼 I'm currently interested in generative AI, data science, data engineering, and software engineering for the enhancement of business processes. 🔗 If you seek advice on implementing GenAI tools in your projects, have questions about best practices, or are simply a like-minded enthusiast with a passion for GenAI, please feel free to connect and reach out!

results.title: CarMax AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
CarMax, the nation's largest used car retailer, has positioned itself as a leader in automotive AI transformation through a comprehensive multi-year digital evolution[1]. The company has deployed AI across customer experience, associate enablement, and operations, with its digital capabilities now supporting over 80% of retail sales[2]. CarMax's AI initiatives include the AI-powered virtual assistant Skye, which has achieved over 50% independent customer question resolution with a 20% year-over-year improvement[3], and Rhodes, an internal knowledge management system for associates[4]. The company expects its AI and technology investments to drive at least $150 million in cost savings over 18 months while improving customer experience and operational efficiency[5].
...

highlights:

...
carmax)
...
#### Manager, Data Science (Current)
...
#### Senior Data Scientist
...
Designing, building, deploying, and maintaining machine learning models for purchasing cars from auctions. Created new algorithms and processes during the Covid-19 pandemic that increased buyer efficiency, allowing our associates to better adapt and redeploy during rapidly changing business conditions throughout the pandemic. Since mid-2021, moved from auctions team to production planning team to provide critical relief to our stores. Responsible for creating car production and logistics plans each week, coordinating between multiple carmax teams to make needed changes to adapt to a changing business environment. In addition, leading a push to upgrade technology tools and processes to streamline and automate our plans, predictions, and reporting.
...
Selected as a data science fellow for prestigious data science fellowship Built and deployed a web app (www.thehairforyou.com) to recommend salons for the user based on their particular needs and hair color. Deployed machine learning algorithms, namely NLP sentiment analysis with VADER and utilizing transfer learning from a CNN with fast.ai's ResNet50 architecture. Scraped 20k+ reviews from Yelp, preprocessed them for analysis, and built a custom scoring algorithm to score them based on user-input keywords in the reviews while minimizing bias in reviews (such as when a review would negatively talk about a different salon). Hand-labelled nearly 3k photos from salon-like hashtags to create a image classifier for 5 hairtypes. Prioritized photos without faces to remove any bias based on facial features for hair classification. Achieved over 90% accuracy in validation data. Built on existing skills gained during PhD (analysis techniques, mathematical and statistical modeling, coding skills) with skills gained during Insight (data science tech stack, machine learning, product sense)

Mentorship has an API now. When shouldn’t you call it? 

The best mentorship strategy isn't choosing between AI and a human - it's knowing how to use both. AI gives you unlimited, tireless access, zero judgment, and instant feedback. A human gives you accountability, lived experience, and an irreplaceable relationship. 

I recorded a version of the 5 minute lightning talk I delivered on this topic at the RVAtech Data and AI Summit in March 2026 here: https://lnkd.in/eVmiNKMw In it, I break down the strengths and real limits of both AI and human mentors, and share some tips for building out a system of human and AI mentors and coaches that works for you.
...
What stuck with me from the Richmond Technology Council - rvatech/ Data + AI Summit?
...
- Andrew Novokhatny presented a fun and interesting case study of parameter efficient fine tuning of an LLM. He also provided great framing for why you would choose different fine tuning methods vs. RAG depending on your use case, and how to approach fine tuning with LoRA. 

- Alden Geipel shared The Doctor Strange Approach to Parallel Orchestration. He grounded us in the shift from prompt engineering to context engineering to environment engineering, and then his engaging demo proposed ways to design the world that agents live in and define how they work together for software development.

It was a perfectly timed message with my recent career move. And after spending a day immersed in the Richmond tech community, I think we have the right ingredients to “be bold, be brave, be benevolent” in this moment. 🔥

highlights:

# Associate CASS Data Scientist
...
As an Associate CASS Data Scientist, you’ll help drive the analytical backbone of Southwest’s Continuing Analysis and Surveillance System (CASS), turning complex operational data into insights that strengthen safety, compliance, and reliability. You’ll develop automated workflows, build models, and apply advanced analytics—including machine learning and AI—to detect trends, assess risk, and optimize the health of our Continuous Airworthiness Maintenance Program. Partnering closely with Tech Ops, CASS Leadership, and cross‑functional stakeholders, you’ll create dashboards and predictive tools that inform critical decisions and support the ongoing improvement of our maintenance programs. By blending strong analytical methods with real‑world operational understanding, you’ll play a key role in ensuring our fleet stays safe, efficient, and ready to serve our Customers.

## Job description
...
Google's software engineers develop the next-generation technologies that change how billions of users connect, explore, and interact with information and one another. Our products need to handle information at massive scale, and extend well beyond web search. We're looking for engineers who bring fresh ideas from all areas, including information retrieval, distributed computing, large-scale system design, networking and data storage, security, artificial intelligence, natural language processing, UI design and mobile; the list goes on and is growing every day. As a software engineer, you will work on a specific project critical to Google’s needs with opportunities to switch teams and projects as you and our fast-paced business grow and evolve. We need our engineers to be versatile, display leadership qualities and be enthusiastic to take on new problems across the full-stack as we continue to push technology forward.
...
As a Software Engineer on the Shopping Ads team, you will be an "AI First" engineer dedicated to solving complex issues using Generative AI . You will design, implement, and launch innovative Large Language Model (LLM) and Machine Learning (ML) systems to enhance the shopping journey for users worldwide.
...
